_comment: Generic fixture exercising the typed 'stage' node model used by lifecycle maps. The nodeType 'stage' layout renders an objective (Prose), a meta table (KeyValue), a 'Features' Section listing module objects as Tiles, a 'States' Section listing state objects as Tiles, and 'Today'/'Tomorrow' Sections each wrapping a Prose narrative. Used by typed-stage-map.test.tsx to prove the stage layout resolves features/states/narratives as real text (never 'Unknown' or an unresolved '$binding') through the real app pipeline. Content is invented and illustrative only.

meta.title: Generic Lifecycle
meta.subtitle: stage-based flow
meta.context: Each stage owns an objective, its features, and its states.

context.objective: The stage where the platform turns completed work into recognized revenue and gets paid for it.

modules.name: Invoicing engine
modules.tomorrow: L4 issues correct invoices automatically the moment work is confirmed

modules.name: Payment capture
modules.tomorrow: L4 collects funds with zero manual follow-up across methods

context.todayNarrative: Today this stage runs on spreadsheets and manual chase-ups, so revenue lands late and reconciliation eats the week.
context.tomorrowNarrative: Tomorrow invoicing and capture run themselves, and the team only touches the exceptions.

context.objective: The stage where new work is received, validated, and queued for the revenue stage.

modules.name: Order validation
modules.tomorrow: L4 rejects malformed orders before they ever reach revenue

context.todayNarrative: Today intake is a shared inbox someone watches.
context.tomorrowNarrative: Tomorrow intake validates and routes itself.
